signature: string join(list: list<string>, delimiter: string?) : string
description: return a string which is composed by joining all the string elements from the list parameter, separated by the delimiter. The delimiter can be an empty string. Null elements in the list parameter are ignored. If list is empty, the result is the empty string. If delimiter is null, the string elements are joined without a separator.

Returns the median element of the list of numbers. I.e., after sorting the list, if the list has an odd number of elements, it returns the middle element. If the list has an even number of elements, returns the average of the two middle elements. If the list is empty, returns null.

Returns the median element of the list of numbers. I.e., after sorting the list, if the list has an odd number of elements, it returns the middle element. If the list has an even number of elements, returns the average of the two middle elements. If the list is empty, returns null.
Returns the sample standard deviation of the list of numbers. If the list is empty or if the list contains only one element, the function returns null.
Returns the sample standard deviation of the list of numbers. If the list is empty or if the list contains only one element, the function returns null.
Returns the mode of the list of numbers. If the result contains multiple elements, they are returned in ascending order. If the list is empty, an empty list is returned.
Returns the mode of the list of numbers. If the result contains multiple elements, they are returned in ascending order. If the list is empty, an empty list is returned.

produces a list of key,value pairs from a context m

signature: get value(m: context, key: string) : Any
description: select the value of the entry named key from context m

Returns a new context that includes all specified entries. If a context item contains additional entries beyond the required "key" and "value" entries, the additional entries are ignored. If a context item is missing the required "key" and "value" entries, the final result is null. See also: get entries() builtin function.

signature: context put(context: context, key: string, value: Any) : context
description: (a) Returns a new context that includes the new entry, or overriding the existing value if an entry for the same key already exists in the supplied context parameter. A new entry is added as the last entry of the new context. If overriding an existing entry, the order of the keys maintains the same order as in the original context.

signature: context put(context: context, keys: list<string>, value: Any) : context
description: Returns the composite of nested invocations to context put() for each item in keys hierarchy in context. If keys is a list of 1 element, this is equivalent to context put(context, key', value), where key' is the only element in the list keys. If keys is a list of 2 or more elements, this is equivalent of calling context put(context, key', value'), with: key' is the head element in the list keys, value' is the result of invocation of context put(context', keys', value), where: context' is the result of context.key', keys' is the remainder of the list keys without the head element key'. If keys is an empty list or null, the result is null.

Returns a new context that includes all entries from the given contexts; if some of the keys are equal, the entries are overriden. The entries are overridden in the same order as specified by the supplied parameter, with new entries added as the last entry in the new context.
Evaluates to true if and only if point1 < point2
Evaluates to true if and only if point < range.start or (point = range.start and not(range.start included) )
Evaluates to true if and only if range.end < point or (range.end = point and not(range.end included) )
Evaluates to true if and only if range1.end < range2.start or (( not(range1.end included) or not(range2.start included)) and range 1 .end = range2.start)
Evaluates to true if and only if point1 > point2
Evaluates to true if and only if point > range.end or (point = range.end and not(range.end included) )
Evaluates to true if and only if range.start > point or (range.start = point and not(range.start included) )
Evaluates to true if and only if range 1 .start > range2.end or (( not(range1 .start included) or not(range2.end included) ) and range 1 .start = range2.end)
Evaluates to true if and only if range1.end included and range2.start included and range 1 .end = range2.start

signature: met by(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.start included and range2.end included and range 1 .start = range2.end

Evaluates to true if and only if (range1.end > range2.start or (range1.end = range2.start and range1.end included and range2.start included)) and (range1.start < range2.end or (range1.start = range2.end and range1.start included and range2.end included))

signature: overlaps before(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range1.start < range2.start or (range1.start = range2.start and range1.start included and not(range2.start included))) and (range1.end > range2.start or (range1.end = range2.start and range1.end included and range2.start included)) and (range1.end < range2.end or (range1.end = range2.end and (not(range1.end included) or range2.end included )))

signature: overlaps after(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range2.start < range1.start or (range2.start = range1.start and range2.start included and not( range 1.start included))) and (range2.end > range 1.start or (range2.end = range 1.start and range2.end included and range 1.start included )) and (range2.end < range1.end or (range2.end = range1.end and (not(range2.end included) or range1.end included)))
Evaluates to true if and only if range.end included and range.end = point

Evaluates to true if and only if range1.end included = range2.end included and range1.end = range2.end and (range1.start > range2.start or (range1.start = range2.start and (not(range1.start included) or range2.start included)))

signature: finished by(range: range<comparable>, point: comparable) : boolean
description: Evaluates to true if and only if range.end included and range.end = point

signature: finished by(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.end included = range2.end included and range1.end = range2.end and (range1.start < range2.start or (range1.start = range2.start and (range1.start included or not(range2.start included))))
Evaluates to true if and only if (range.start < point and range.end > point) or (range.start = point and range.start included) or (range.end = point and range.end included)

Evaluates to true if and only if (range1.start < range2.start or (range1.start = range2.start and (range1.start included or not(range2.start included)))) and (range1.end > range2.end or (range1.end = range2.end and (range1.end included or not(range2.end included))))
Evaluates to true if and only if (range.start < point and range.end > point) or (range.start = point and range.start included) or (range.end = point and range.end included)

Evaluates to true if and only if (range2.start < range1.start or (range2.start = range1.start and (range2.start included or not(range1.start included)))) and (range2.end > range1.end or (range2.end = range1.end and (range2.end included or not(range1.end included))))
Evaluates to true if and only if range.start = point and range.start included

Evaluates to true if and only if range1.start = range2.start and range1.start included = range2.start included and (range1.end < range2.end or (range1.end = range2.end and (not(range1.end included) or range2.end included)))

signature: started by(range: range<comparable>, point: comparable) : boolean
description: Evaluates to true if and only if range.start = point and range.start included

signature: started by(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.start = range2.start and range1.start included = range2.start included and (range2.end < range1.end or (range2.end = range1.end and (not(range2.end included) or range1.end included)))
Evaluates to true if and only if point1 = point2
Evaluates to true if and only if range1.start = range2.start and range1.start included = range2.start included and range1.end = range2.end and range1.end included = range2.end included
